author: Dr. Sameer Iqbal (Interventional Radiology)
content:
IR PROCEDURE NOTE — UNIVERSITY MEDICAL CENTER
Date: 3/10/2027

PROCEDURES: (1) Ultrasound-guided large-volume therapeutic paracentesis. (2) Ultrasound-guided right therapeutic thoracentesis.
Operator: Dr. Sameer Iqbal (IR).

PRE-PROCEDURE LABS: INR 2.0, platelets 55 K (chronic, from cirrhosis).

BLOOD-PRODUCT MANAGEMENT: No FFP or platelet transfusion was administered. Albumin 25% was given as 8 g per liter of ascitic fluid removed (for large-volume paracentesis).

PARACENTESIS: 6.5 L of clear straw-colored ascitic fluid removed. Fluid sent for cell count/differential, albumin, and culture. (Result: SAAG >1.1 g/dL consistent with portal hypertension; PMN <250/uL — no spontaneous bacterial peritonitis.)

THORACENTESIS: 1.3 L of serous transudative fluid removed from the right pleural space under ultrasound guidance. Post-procedure imaging without pneumothorax.

COMPLICATIONS: None. No bleeding. The patient's breathing and abdominal distension returned to baseline; discharged home.

PLAN: Symptomatic recurrence is expected given her physiology. Given her distance from the center (90 minutes) and her sister's availability to drive, outpatient drainage closer to home was discussed with hepatology.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Sameer Iqbal, MD (Interventional Radiology). 3/10/2027.
